title: Dead Start/Stop Station
workOrder: WORK ORDER #101 — Conveyor won't start. Operator: "I hit Start and nothing happens." That's all you get.
symptom: The conveyor won't start. It's a DIFFERENT fault each time you open this ticket — don't assume, don't parts-cannon. Meter it out, localize it, then make the call.

explains.FU1:blown_fuse: You read 24V ACROSS FU1 — voltage in on TP-L, none out on TP-1. A good fuse reads ~0V across it; full source voltage across it means it's OPEN. The whole station was dark because nothing downstream had power to even be tested. Replace it — then find WHY it blew, or the new one pops too.
explains.CR1:open_coil: 24V was sitting right on the coil (TP-coil to N) but it never pulled in, and the Stopped light stayed on. Voltage present with no magnetic pull = the coil winding is open (burned out). Replace the coil / relay.
explains.STOP:stuck_open: 24V at the fuse output (TP-1) but 0V the instant you crossed the Stop button = the normally-closed Stop contact is stuck OPEN. It breaks the rung before Start ever gets a vote — nothing can latch. (The Stopped light still glows because it feeds off TP-1, ahead of the Stop button.)
explains.START:broken_wire: Power sat waiting at the Start button's line side, but pressing Start put nothing through to the coil — the Start contact or its wire is open. With no initial pulse, the seal-in never gets a chance to grab and hold.
explains.SEAL:stuck_open: Classic: it RAN while you held Start, then died the instant you let go. The CR1 seal-in contact isn't making, so nothing keeps the coil energized once the Start button opens. The latch has no latch.
